title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While driving to Oxford Street, Bandel and Anthony discuss Virginia. Bandel claims to have inside information regarding Virginia’s marriage to the late Tim Ruff. She asserts that Virginia never loved Tim, despite his intense passion and threats of suicide, and that Virginia only married him because of his overwhelming devotion. Bandel notes that Virginia is highly intelligent, helped Tim's career in Hersovlav, and deeply values her freedom. After Bandel drops Anthony off at the Hyde Park corner, Anthony takes a taxi to Victoria Station and eventually travels to Dover.

Upon arriving in Dover, Anthony seeks directions to Hirstmill, the last house on Langleys Lane. He finds the house abandoned and secluded, surrounded by overgrown gardens. While approaching the property stealthily, Anthony observes a short, stocky foreign man acting as a guard. He follows the guard to an open area and peers through a window, discovering six men gathered around a table. The group consists of four robust men with Magyar features and two small, agile men. The men speak French and engage in a heated argument regarding their "boss," whom they are waiting for. One man identifies the leader as Victor, noting that Victor does not tolerate defiance. The men also mention a prisoner whose head wound has healed. Anthony, observing the group's lack of discipline and the tension between the different factions, decides he wants to give "King Victor's" headquarters a lesson.
